claim: Provider configuration in demand evidence dispatch uses provider-specific CLI argument patterns and does not pass tool restriction flags to any provider, relying on boundary enforcement instead.

evidence.summary: Codex provider invocation uses distinct CLI structure: --cd, --sandbox, -c for approval_policy, --output-last-message, no tool flags.
evidence.why: Shows Codex has no tool configuration parameters in its dispatch contract, only sandbox and approval policy.

evidence.summary: Custom provider invocation uses shell template command with ${model} substitution, no tool flags.
evidence.why: Shows custom provider has no tool configuration parameters, only command template.
Shows Claude has tool restriction parameters available in its dispatch contract.

evidence.summary: executionConfig normalizes agent_tool_profile and sets Claude-specific flags: settings='', claude_tools='', claude_allowed_tools='', claude_disallowed_tools='', claude_no_session_persistence=true.
evidence.why: Evidence mode explicitly clears Claude tool restriction flags regardless of config.ai input, preventing cross-provider flag leakage.

evidence.summary: Codex provider special case: if provider is codex and config.ai.model contains 'claude', model field is cleared to avoid cross-provider model pollution.
evidence.why: Shows explicit handling to prevent Claude default model assumptions from leaking into Codex dispatch.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot and diffBoundarySnapshots implement git-backed file mutation detection with allowed roots exclusion.
evidence.why: Boundary enforcement is provider-agnostic and works by comparing before/after file digests regardless of which provider was used.

evidence.summary: Boundary violations after agent execution result in BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers and force prd_ready=false.
evidence.why: Shows boundary violations are hard blockers regardless of which provider caused them.

evidence.summary: Test 'codex dispatch does not inherit a claude default model or force read-only sandbox' verifies provider isolation.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirms Codex dispatch clears Claude model defaults and does not force sandbox assumptions.

evidence.summary: Test 'claude dispatch does not pass tool restriction flags and relies on boundary enforcement' verifies tool flags are cleared.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirms Claude tool restriction flags are empty in evidence mode, boundary handles mutation detection.

evidence.summary: agent_tool_profile normalization: research/fetch/web/web-research/external-research normalize to 'research'; full/all/write/execution normalize to 'full' only with allowFullAgentTools=true; default is 'boundary'.
evidence.why: Shows evidence mode has three tool profiles, and 'full' requires explicit authorization to prevent accidental tool access.

evidence.summary: PROVIDER_ALIASES map normalizes provider names: anthropic/anthropic→claude, openai→codex, shell/local→custom.
evidence.why: Shows provider normalization layer prevents alias drift before provider invocation logic.

evidence.summary: buildAgentAdapterCapabilities returns provider-specific capability contracts: Codex has sandbox/approval, Claude has permission-mode, custom has external mode.
evidence.why: Shows each provider has distinct capability contract with different sandbox/approval/tool assumptions.

assumptions: Codex and custom providers do not have tool restriction APIs equivalent to Claude's --tools/allowedTools/disallowedTools flags.
Boundary enforcement assumes git is available and working for git-backed snapshot; falls back to filesystem walk otherwise.
Provider command availability (codex, claude CLI on PATH) is assumed but verified by agent-contract inspector before execution.
Evidence agent tool profile setting persists across provider switching if not explicitly reset in executionConfig.
risks: If a provider other than Claude gains tool restriction flags, the current clearing logic (lines 456-462) would not apply to them.
If git is not available or repository state is dirty, boundary detection may miss changes or have false positives.
Model value clearing for Codex (line 448-449) uses string matching 'claude' which could be overly aggressive or miss other model patterns.

result.notes: Provider configuration correctly uses provider-specific CLI patterns and clears cross-provider assumptions. Evidence mode does not pass tool restriction flags to any provider, relying on boundary enforcement instead. Tests confirm provider isolation.
